skill.name: api-client
skill.description: Guides API client setup with TanStack Query, Axios, JWT interceptors, error handling, Zod parsing, pagination, and offline-aware requests.
skill.summary: Build robust React API clients with typed requests, caching, retries, JWT handling, and response validation.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network use, environment access, weak crypto, and a critical heuristic combination. Review found a documentation-only skill with TypeScript and bash examples; no executable script, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious instruction was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The referenced lines are Markdown fenced code blocks and template literals inside examples, not runnable Ruby or shell execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms the hits are documentation delimiters and TypeScript examples in SKILL.md. There is no separate script file or instruction to execute untrusted shell commands.

low_findings.title: Expected API Networking Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are expected for an API client guide. They show Axios imports, example base URLs, and official documentation links without sending secrets to an unauthorized endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network usage is directly aligned with the skill purpose and uses local placeholders or reference URLs. No evidence found of hidden callbacks or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Public Environment Variable Documentation
low_findings.description: The environment findings document NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and an example .env.local entry. No secret environment variable or environment file read is performed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines only configure a public API base URL for generated application code. The skill text does not ask the model to read local secrets or upload environment contents.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not correspond to hashing or encryption APIs in the reviewed line context. JWT is discussed as application authentication guidance, not custom cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines include metadata, toast messages, and API-client examples rather than weak algorithms such as MD5 or SHA1. The assessment is based on the static locations provided.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Match Is Benign
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to an enabled guard for a student identifier in a query example. It does not inspect host state, enumerate files, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a React Query enabled condition that checks whether an id exists. No system command, host fingerprinting, or reconnaissance behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Critical Heuristic Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner combined documentation examples for code fences, networking, and environment variables into a critical heuristic. Manual review found no executable malicious chain or credential access behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combined indicators exist only in instructional Markdown examples. No evidence found that the skill executes code, reads credentials, or transmits sensitive data.

content.value_statement: React apps need consistent API access, caching, retries, and token handling. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a typed client using TanStack Query, Axios, and Zod.
actual_capabilities: Configures TanStack Query defaults for caching, retries, stale time, and reconnect behavior.
Defines an Axios client with base URL, timeout, JWT attachment, refresh handling, and typed methods.
Creates reusable query and mutation hooks for React and TypeScript applications.
Adds Zod response schemas for students, attendance records, and paginated API responses.
Shows pagination, infinite queries, optimistic updates, rollback, offline checks, and abortable requests.
Documents expected files, integration points, and quality checks for API client work.
limitations: The skill provides implementation guidance and examples, not a ready-made npm package.
Examples assume React, TypeScript, TanStack Query, Axios, Zod, and a compatible auth store.
Endpoint names use student and attendance examples that must be adapted to each backend.
Security settings such as token storage and refresh rotation still require project-specific review.

use_cases.title: Set up a new client layer
use_cases.description: Create shared API client files, query defaults, typed hooks, and consistent request behavior for a React app.

use_cases.title: Standardize authenticated requests
use_cases.description: Attach bearer tokens, handle 401 responses, refresh sessions, and redirect users when authentication fails.

use_cases.title: Improve data reliability
use_cases.description: Add validation, retries, pagination, optimistic updates, rollback, and offline-aware behavior to user-facing workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic API client
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a React project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-client skill to set up TanStack Query and an Axios client for my React TypeScript app.

prompt_templates.title: Add authenticated requests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-client skill to add JWT bearer token attachment, 401 handling, token refresh, and login redirect behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build typed feature hooks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-client skill to create typed hooks with Zod validation for a paginated list and detail view.

prompt_templates.title: Design resilient mutations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-client skill to add optimistic updates, rollback on error, query invalidation, offline checks, and abortable requests.

output_examples.input: Set up API fetching for a student dashboard.
output_examples.output: A file plan for query configuration, an authenticated client, typed schemas, reusable hooks, and dashboard-specific data hooks.

output_examples.input: Handle expired sessions in API calls.
output_examples.output: A response flow that refreshes the token, retries the failed request, signs out on refresh failure, and redirects to login.
A mutation plan with optimistic cache updates, a saved previous value, rollback on failure, and refetch after completion.

best_practices: Validate API responses with Zod before using data in components.
Keep API clients, schemas, feature hooks, and UI components in separate files.
Document caching, retry, and invalidation decisions with clear project rationale.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter raw fetch calls across components when shared hooks are needed.
Do not trust API response shapes without validation in critical workflows.
Do not store or forward tokens without reviewing the project authentication model.

faq.question: Does this skill create a complete API client package?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for generating project files inside an existing React TypeScript app.

faq.question: Which data fetching library does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers TanStack Query v5 for caching, retries, invalidation, pagination, and mutation workflows.

faq.question: Can it use Axios interceptors?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes an Axios instance with request and response interceptors for JWT-based APIs.

faq.question: Does it validate response data?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use Zod schemas to parse and validate API responses before returning typed data.

faq.question: Is it limited to student and attendance APIs?
faq.answer: No. Those are examples. The same patterns can be adapted to other backend resources.

faq.question: Does it handle offline and cancellation behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for online status checks and AbortController-based request cancellation.
